channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Gadkari raises concerns over use of Environment Compensation Cess to curb Delhi pollution
item.description: New Delhi: Union Road Transport Minister Nitin Gadkari on Monday expressed concerns over the effectiveness of the Environment Compensation Cess (ECC) collected from vehicles in addressing Delhi’s seve...

item.title: Alberta Judge Approves Class-Action Suit Over Exshaw Cement Plant Emissions
item.description: A judge in Calgary, Alberta, has officially certified a class-action lawsuit against the operators of the Exshaw cement plant, allowing affected residents to collectively pursue legal action. The laws...

item.title: Senator Bato dela Rosa appeals to President Marcos over ICC warrant
item.description: Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa has publicly appealed to President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. not to execute the arrest warrant issued against him by the International Criminal Court (ICC). The warrant rela...

item.title: Canberra Women's Rugby Team Calls for Dedicated Change Rooms to Support Growth and Safety
item.description: At Easts Rugby Club in Canberra, the introduction of a women's 10s team in 2025 has sparked enthusiasm among players like Lilly Scott, whose family has a three-generation history with the club. Despit...

item.title: Vijay engages with AIADMK rebels amid Shanmugam's support for TVK
item.description: Actor and political figure Vijay recently met with certain members of the AIADMK party who have expressed discontent with the current leadership. This meeting comes at a time when intra-party tensions...

item.title: The Wiggles admit limited knowledge of Oasis and favour Blur
item.description: During an appearance on Nova 96.9’s Ricki-Lee & Tim radio show, Australian children’s music group The Wiggles revealed that they are not familiar with the Britpop band Oasis. Members Anthony and Lucia...

item.title: Kenya Targets Gold in Women’s 5,000m at 2026 African Athletics Championships
item.description: Kenya is set to kick off its medal chase at the 2026 African Athletics Championships in Accra, Ghana, with the women’s 5,000 metres. Dorcas Chepkwemoi Ndiema, a training mate of Olympic and world 1,50...

item.title: The Plaster Casts of Pompeii: How Archaeologists Preserved the Final Moments of Vesuvius Victims
item.description: The famous figures from Pompeii, often mistaken for petrified human bodies, are actually plaster casts made by archaeologists. When Mount Vesuvius erupted in 79 AD, it buried the Roman city under laye...

item.title: South Africa's Unemployment Rate Climbs to 32.7% in First Quarter of 2026
item.description: According to the latest Quarterly Labour Force Survey released by Statistics South Africa on Tuesday, the country's unemployment rate increased by 1.3 percentage points to 32.7% in the first quarter o...

item.title: AFP Monitors 35 Chinese Vessels in West Philippine Sea Features This Week
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) reported monitoring a total of 35 Chinese vessels in different key areas of the West Philippine Sea from May 4 to 11. According to AFP spokesperson Rear Admir...

item.title: Reflections on Motherhood and Life Abroad
item.description: This article shares the personal experiences of Fortunata Kitokesya, a Tanzanian mother and human rights expert, as she navigates motherhood while studying and interning abroad. She describes the emot...

item.title: Rare trillium bloom draws hikers to protected Virginia forest
item.description: A small group of hikers gathered early on a chilly May morning to take part in the Trillium Trek, a guided nature walk through Virginia’s G. Richard Thompson Wildlife Management Area. This remote 4,00...

item.title: Kenya Police Detain Multiple Protesters Near Africa Forward Summit Venues
item.description: On Tuesday, Nairobi saw heightened security around the Africa Forward Summit as police arrested at least 11 demonstrators attempting to reach the summit venues. Anti-riot units were stationed around k...

item.title: Melvin Jerusalem Confident Ahead of Rematch with Siyakholwa Kuse in South Africa
item.description: Filipino boxer Melvin 'El Gringo' Jerusalem is set to defend his WBC mini-flyweight world title in South Africa against Siyakholwa Kuse, giving up the usual home advantage. Jerusalem previously defeat...

item.title: U.S. Lawmakers Raise Concerns Over Canada’s Trade and Security Ties with China
item.description: Several prominent U.S. Democrats have expressed concern over Canada’s increasing engagement with China, warning that it may complicate North American security and trade relations. Michigan Senator Eli...

item.title: Abuja to host CENSUS’26 to empower women for leadership and societal impact
item.description: Women from all corners of Nigeria go gather for CENSUS’26, one major faith-based programme wey dey aim to empower women for leadership, spiritual growth, and societal transformation. The event wey Ext...

item.title: Tanzanite Queens make history with Women's Cup qualification, praised by Samia
item.description: The President of Tanzania, Samia Suluhu Hassan, has publicly commended the Tanzanite Queens following their historic qualification for the Women's Cup. This achievement marks a major milestone in Tanz...

item.title: Trump Denies Republican Warnings Against Military Action in Cuba
item.description: President Donald Trump publicly denied reports that Senate Republicans had cautioned him against ordering military strikes on Cuba. The 79-year-old president issued a 40-word statement on Truth Social...

item.title: A Ranked List of the Top 100 English-Language Novels
item.description: The Guardian has compiled a list of the 100 greatest novels ever published in English, as determined by a global panel of authors, critics, and academics. This ranking aims to celebrate literary achie...

item.title: New CNN Poll Shows Declining Public Confidence in Trump’s Economic Leadership
item.description: A new CNN poll conducted by SSRS suggests growing public dissatisfaction with President Donald Trump’s handling of the U.S. economy, an issue that has historically been one of his strongest political ...
